description: Ground-level photograph depicting a cultivated agricultural field on a gentle slope, identified as Śniadków Dolny, Poland. The field contains numerous young trees or saplings planted in distinct, regular linear rows extending into the distance. Individual trees are protected by dark-coloured sleeves at their bases. The ground consists primarily of light brown, exposed soil, interspersed with sparse patches of green vegetation, likely grasses or weeds. The sky above is a pale blue with some diffuse white clouds. This scene is consistent with a young fruit tree plantation or olive grove, relating to Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS) nomenclature classes 2.2.1.0 (Vineyards, fruit trees and berry plantations) or 2.2.2.0 (Olive groves). The image is dated 2013-08-06 and sourced from CNES 2013©, Distribution Airbus DS/Spot Image. The accompanying metadata in the document mentions "Spot 6 (1.5 m) (4/1/3 Band Combination)", suggesting this ground photograph serves as ground truth or a visual reference for interpreting corresponding satellite imagery in Land Use / Land Cover (LULC) classification.
